principles: Treat Codex and Claude as provider tabs.
Use one provider-neutral ThreadPage with provider-specific adapters.
Avoid board, loop, team, mailbox, and insights features.
Prefer local-first indexing and tolerant parsers over unofficial provider internals.
Do not require Python, FastAPI, a local HTTP port, or user-managed dependency installation.
Optimize for immediate first paint, cached summaries, fast jumps, and isolated provider failures.
Store AgentHUD-owned state as rebuildable JSON with schema versions.
Keep provider capabilities explicit so unsupported actions never appear as broken buttons.

milestones.title: Extraction Decision
milestones.goal: Choose the AgentHUD package boundary and preserve the current product while planning the clean fork.

milestones.title: Pure Codex Thread Page
milestones.goal: Ship a clean Codex-only Thread page without board, loop, team, or insights.

milestones.title: Provider Contract
milestones.goal: Introduce provider-neutral thread and detail contracts.

milestones.title: Claude Local Indexer
milestones.goal: Read Claude transcript JSONL into the provider contract.

milestones.title: Unified Provider Tabs
milestones.goal: Expose Codex and Claude as tabs of the same Thread page.

milestones.title: Hardening
milestones.goal: Make indexing, refresh, and UI behavior reliable on large histories.

milestones.title: Polish and Extensibility
milestones.goal: Add search, source reveal, privacy, provider health, keyboard flow, and future-provider polish without expanding into board/team mode.

tasks.title: Choose AgentHUD package boundary
tasks.description: Decide whether AgentHUD is a new extension package, a branch, or an isolated subtree inside this repo.
acceptance_criteria: Package identity and directory boundary are documented.
The current Codex-Managed-Agent feature surface is not broken.
The chosen boundary makes board, loop, team, and insights optional to omit.
The chosen boundary supports a single VSIX install with no Python service.
evidence: Created docs/agenthud/package-boundary.md documenting AgentHUD as an isolated VS Code extension package rooted at /home/clashuser/hzh/work_bo/agent_ui/agent_hud.
Documented recovered Codex-Managed-Agent source as read-only reference, preserving the current feature surface.

Documented zero-service runtime: VS Code extension host, webview postMessage, local Node file indexers, and rebuildable JSON cache; no Python/FastAPI/local HTTP/native sqlite/post-install dependency requirement.
Documented pure Thread-page feature boundary and explicitly omitted board, loop board, team, mailbox, coordination, usage insights, and vibe dashboards.

tasks.title: Define pure feature allowlist
tasks.description: Write the exact keep/remove list for pure AgentHUD so implementation agents do not re-add original dashboard features.
acceptance_criteria: Thread list, detail, history, search, filters, provider tabs, and native-open actions are explicitly in scope.
Board, loop, team, mailbox, insights, and coordination are explicitly out of scope.
Destructive actions are separately gated by provider capability.
Fast first paint, fast jumps, and isolated provider errors are explicitly in scope.
evidence: Created docs/agenthud/feature-allowlist.md documenting the allowed provider-tabbed Thread page surface: thread list, detail, history, search, filters, provider tabs, quick switch, source reveal, and native-open actions.

Documented out-of-scope feature families including board, loop board, team, mailbox, handoff/coordination, usage reports, insights dashboard, topic maps, word clouds, vibe advice, memory cards, and service start/probe UI.
Documented destructive and mutating actions as provider-capability gated: renameThread, archiveThread, restoreThread, and future delete-like actions.
Documented fast first paint, cache-first hydration, fast jumps, isolated provider failures, background refresh, and large-history safety as required behavior.

tasks.title: Map current code to removable modules
tasks.description: Produce a removal map for modules and message types that belong to board, loop, team, mailbox, insights, and memory cards.
acceptance_criteria: Every removed feature family has a file/function/message-type list.
Thread-page dependencies are separated from removable feature dependencies.
No source file is edited during this analysis task.
evidence: Created docs/agenthud/removal-map.md mapping recovered board, loop/auto-loop, team/mailbox/coordination, insights/usage/vibe, memory cards, and service/dashboard feature families to excluded files, functions, UI elements, and message types.

Separated Thread-page concepts that may be reimplemented, including drawer shape, thread explorer layout, Codex native open, source reveal, provider-scoped routing, and local Node indexer data-source references.

Documented implementation rules for future copying: remove dashboard imports/modules, replace dashboard payload assumptions with provider contract objects, keep native/source actions capability-gated, and validate forbidden names.
Recovered source extension was used as read-only reference only; no source files were edited.

tasks.title: Create minimal AgentHUD VS Code contribution set
tasks.description: Define the minimal package commands, views, configuration, and activation events for opening AgentHUD without any start-server workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Only AgentHUD open, refresh, reveal provider, and quick-switch commands remain.
The webview opens directly to the Thread page.
No board/team/loop/insights commands are contributed.
No command implies Python, FastAPI, or a local HTTP server.
evidence: Created package.json for the standalone AgentHUD VS Code extension with only four contributed commands: agenthud.open, agenthud.refresh, agenthud.revealProvider, and agenthud.quickSwitch.
Contributed only AgentHUD thread webview surfaces: activity bar view agenthud.threads, panel view agenthud.panel, and editor webview panel opened by agenthud.open.
Added extension.js as the minimal activation entry and src/host/panel-view.js as the minimal host runtime for opening a Thread-page shell with Codex and Claude provider tabs.
The opened webview renders AgentHUD Threads directly with provider tabs, search/filter placeholders, a selected-detail area, and refresh wiring; no landing page or dashboard surface is introduced.
No dependencies, scripts, service startup commands, Python/FastAPI/local HTTP settings, or board/team/loop/insight commands are present in the package contribution set.
Added media/agenthud.svg as a bundled static view-container icon asset.

tasks.title: Build Codex Node thread data adapter
tasks.description: Implement a Codex adapter in the VS Code extension host that reads local Codex session/rollout files directly and conforms to the provider contract.
acceptance_criteria: Codex list fetch returns normalized thread rows without calling FastAPI.
Codex detail fetch returns normalized messages and recent events from local files.
Existing Codex open editor/sidebar behavior remains possible through the adapter.
evidence: Added src/host/providers/codex.js implementing a pure Node Codex provider adapter that discovers rollout-*.jsonl files under a configurable sessions directory, defaulting to ~/.codex/sessions.

listThreads returns normalized Codex thread rows with provider, id, title, cwd, status, createdAt, updatedAt, model, sourcePath, messageCount, and preview without FastAPI, HTTP, Python, native sqlite, or package dependencies.

getThreadDetail returns normalized messages, recent events, provider-local parse/read errors, source metadata, and capability flags with openNative true and unsupported mutating/live capabilities false.
Missing or non-directory Codex sessions paths return an empty provider result instead of throwing through the caller.
Malformed JSONL lines are skipped and reported as provider-local source errors without failing the whole thread.
Added sanitized fixture coverage in test/fixtures/codex/sessions and src/host/providers/codex.test.js for list/detail normalization, capabilities, malformed-line reporting, and missing thread lookup.

tasks.title: Build pure Codex ThreadPage
tasks.description: Render a clean Codex Thread page using the existing thread explorer and drawer concepts but without feature-heavy dashboard chrome.
acceptance_criteria: Page includes search, sort, status filter, list, selected detail, messages, and recent events.
No board tab, attach board, attach loop, team, handoff, memory card, or insight UI appears.
Selecting a thread loads detail without requiring overview/dashboard state.
Initial Codex list data renders from the Node adapter; cache-first hydration remains deferred to AGHUD-025.
evidence: Refactored src/host/panel-view.js to create a Codex provider instance, load Codex list data from src/host/providers/codex.js, select a default or clicked thread, and load selected detail through the adapter.

Added src/host/thread-page.js as the pure ThreadPage renderer for provider tabs, Codex search, status filter, sort control, thread list, selected detail, normalized conversation messages, and recent events.
Kept Claude as a placeholder tab only; no Claude parser, indexer, or data path was added.
Provider failures and missing detail are rendered inside the Codex pane/detail state rather than through a global failure surface.
No package changes, dependencies, cache-first hydration, persistent provider state, Python/FastAPI/local HTTP services, native sqlite path, or out-of-scope dashboard feature UI were added.

Added src/host/thread-page.test.js fixture smoke coverage proving a sanitized Codex adapter result renders a thread title, detail message, recent events section, and working search/status filter model.

tasks.title: Simplify host message protocol
tasks.description: Reduce webview-to-host messages to provider selection, thread selection, refresh, native open, copy ID, rename/archive if enabled, quick switch, and storage actions.
acceptance_criteria: Removed feature message types are not bound in the pure webview.
Host facade no longer imports loop/team/insight modules.
Unknown message types are ignored safely.
Host messages are request/response shaped so errors can be shown near the affected provider only.
evidence: Added src/host/message-protocol.js defining an allowlisted webview-to-host protocol for provider selection, thread selection, refresh, query/status/sort changes, quick switch, copy thread ID, native-open stub, rename/archive gated stubs, and storage-shaped no-ops.
Refactored src/host/panel-view.js to route webview messages through normalizeWebviewMessage and a single dispatchMessage switch instead of inline if-case growth.
Unknown or invalid message types are ignored safely by the protocol normalizer before dispatch.
Host responses now include requestId, provider, ok, and error fields when the webview supplies a requestId.
Handler failures are written into provider-scoped Codex list/detail error state or Claude placeholder error state before re-rendering.
Added src/host/message-protocol.test.js covering allowed message normalization, sanitization, and unknown/invalid message ignoring.

tasks.title: Define provider-neutral TypeScript-style contracts
tasks.description: Document and implement the common shapes for thread rows, thread detail, messages, events, and provider capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: Contract distinguishes provider ID, normalized status, message history, event previews, and capabilities.
Codex-specific raw fields do not leak into the generic ThreadPage.
Contract supports providers with no live log capability.
evidence: Created docs/agenthud/provider-contract.md documenting TypeScript-style AgentProviderId, AgentThreadStatus, AgentThread, AgentMessage, AgentEvent, AgentCapabilities, ListThreadsResult, AgentThreadDetail, ThreadDetailResult, ProviderIssue, and ProviderSummaryCachePayload shapes.

Documented normalized provider status semantics, message/event boundaries, capability defaults, providers without native-open/live-log capabilities, adapter result metadata, and schema-versioned summary cache payloads.
Documented the current Codex adapter mapping to the provider-neutral contract and kept raw provider transcript fields inside adapter metadata rather than the generic ThreadPage contract.
Added src/host/provider-contract.js as a small dependency-free runtime helper exporting provider IDs, normalized statuses, message roles, default capabilities, normalizers, and shape guards.

Added src/host/provider-contract.test.js smoke coverage for provider/status/role normalization, capability defaults including liveLogs false, valid summary rows, and valid detail results for a provider without live logs.
No ThreadPage/provider refactor, Claude parser, package change, dependency, service/backend, or runtime scaffold was introduced.

tasks.title: Refactor ThreadPage to consume provider contract
tasks.description: Make the UI consume the provider-contract helper and normalized provider data rather than Codex-specific names such as rollout_path, compaction_count, or codexLinkState.
acceptance_criteria: ThreadPage renders from normalized rows and details.
Provider-specific labels and actions are injected from capabilities.
Codex still works through the adapter.
evidence: Refactored src/host/thread-page.js to import provider IDs, normalized statuses, provider ID normalization, thread status normalization, and capability normalization from src/host/provider-contract.js.
ThreadPage status filter options now derive from the provider contract statuses while preserving the existing all/running/recent/idle/archived/unknown UI choices.
Thread rows, search filtering, and detail metadata now normalize provider-specific raw status values to the contract fallback instead of rendering raw adapter-specific status strings.
Thread detail rendering now normalizes capability objects with normalizeCapabilities so missing capability fields default to false and capability-driven native-open UI remains explicit.
Updated src/host/thread-page.test.js to cover partial capability normalization and raw provider status fallback while preserving the existing Codex adapter render path.
No Claude parser, provider persistence, storage migration, native-open depth, package change, dependency, service/backend, or out-of-scope dashboard feature was introduced.

tasks.title: Add provider state persistence
tasks.description: Persist selected provider, selected thread per provider, and search/filter/sort per provider without board state; defer pinned rows until a concrete pure ThreadPage pin UI exists.
acceptance_criteria: Reload preserves the active provider tab.
Switching provider restores that provider's selected thread and filters.
No boardTabAssignments or boardAttached state remains.
State writes are debounced and schema-versioned.
This slice does not implement the generic JSON storage migration/source-manifest layer reserved for AGHUD-026.
evidence: Added src/host/ui-state.js implementing a schema-versioned VS Code globalState UI-state payload under agenthud.uiState with selected provider and per-provider selectedThreadId, query, statusFilter, and sort fields.
UI-state reads tolerate missing, corrupt, and unsupported schema payloads by returning null so runtime construction falls back to createThreadPageState.
Refactored src/host/panel-view.js to apply persisted UI state during runtime construction before first render.
Added debounced UI-state writes for provider selection, thread selection, query/status/sort changes, cached Codex hydration default selection, and refresh-driven selected-thread replacement.
SAVE_UI_STATE now flushes the current schema-versioned UI-state payload and LOAD_UI_STATE reapplies persisted UI state without adding a file-backed storage layer.
Added src/host/ui-state.test.js covering snapshot shape, restore behavior, invalid value normalization, writable fake globalState, and unsupported schema fallback.

No generic JSON storage migration/source-manifest layer, Claude parser/discovery, pin UI, package change, dependency, service/backend, native-open depth, or out-of-scope dashboard feature was introduced.

tasks.title: Discover Claude transcript sources
tasks.description: Implement source discovery for Claude local transcript files under ~/.claude/projects, including nested subagent files as a later optional source.
acceptance_criteria: Discovery returns project directory, JSONL path, session ID, size, mtime, and likely cwd/project label.
Subagent files are detected but excluded from the main list by default.
Discovery handles missing ~/.claude gracefully.
This slice does not parse transcript messages, build the Claude index cache, or wire Claude into the UI.
evidence: Added src/host/providers/claude.js with createClaudeProvider and discoverClaudeSources for discovery-only scanning of a configurable Claude projects root, defaulting to ~/.claude/projects.

Discovery returns normalized Claude source rows with provider, projectDir, path/jsonlPath, sessionId, size, mtimeMs, ISO mtime, projectLabel, likely cwd, isSubagent, and a source-manifest-compatible entry shape.
Nested subagents JSONL files are detected and returned separately in subagentSources while excluded from the main sources list by default; includeSubagents can opt them into sources.
Missing or non-directory Claude projects roots return empty sources/subagentSources with provider-scoped metadata instead of throwing through callers.
Likely cwd and project label are inferred cheaply from Claude's encoded project directory name without parsing conversation messages.
Added sanitized Claude fixture JSONL files under test/fixtures/claude/projects covering main sessions and nested subagent discovery.

Added src/host/providers/claude.test.js covering main discovery rows, subagent exclusion/inclusion, provider wrapper behavior, missing root fallback, cwd decoding, session ID extraction, and manifest entry shape.

No Claude message parsing, Claude hot index cache, source manifest writes from live scans, UI wiring, package change, dependency, service/backend, native-open depth, pin UI, or out-of-scope dashboard feature was introduced.

tasks.title: Build tolerant Claude JSONL parser
tasks.description: Parse Claude JSONL sessions into normalized messages and summary metadata while filtering meta records and noisy wrapper messages.
acceptance_criteria: Parser accepts string content and array content.
Parser recognizes user, assistant, tool_use, tool_result, system, permission-mode, snapshot, and last-prompt records.
Default output hides command caveats, file-history snapshots, permission records, and plugin routing wrappers.
Malformed JSON lines do not fail the whole thread.
This slice does not implement the Claude index cache, provider API wiring, or UI integration.
evidence: Extended src/host/providers/claude.js with parseClaudeSession and tolerant Claude record normalization while keeping discovery and parsing in the same provider module.
The parser accepts string content and array content, extracting visible assistant/user/system text blocks and emitting normalized tool messages for tool_use and tool_result records.
The parser recognizes and hides permission-mode, file-history-snapshot, and last-prompt records by default while tracking hidden record counts in parser metadata.
System wrapper noise is hidden by default when it matches command-caveat or routing-style patterns, while meaningful system records can still produce normalized system messages.
Parser summary metadata now prefers transcript content over path heuristics for cwd, title, preview, createdAt, updatedAt, and model when those fields are present in the session records.
Malformed JSON lines are tolerated and reported in parser metadata errors without failing the whole session parse.

Added src/host/providers/claude-parser.test.js and a richer sanitized fixture session under test/fixtures/claude/projects/-home-user-rich-project/session-rich.jsonl covering array content, tool_use, tool_result, hidden meta records, and malformed JSON.
No Claude index cache, provider API/runtime wiring, UI integration, package change, dependency, service/backend, native-open depth, pin UI, or out-of-scope dashboard feature was introduced.

tasks.title: Implement Claude index cache
tasks.description: Cache Claude parsed summaries by path, size, and mtime so refresh does not rescan every transcript.
acceptance_criteria: Cold scan works on large ~/.claude/projects directories.
Hot refresh reparses only changed files.
Cache invalidates when file size or mtime changes.
Index has a clear storage location and can be rebuilt.
This slice does not wire Claude into panel runtime, ThreadPage UI, or provider API dispatch.
evidence: Extended src/host/providers/claude.js with buildClaudeSummaryIndex and createClaudeProvider().buildSummaryIndex for cache-only Claude parsed-summary indexing.
Implemented cache entries keyed by path/size/mtimeMs through a stable cacheKey, reusing unchanged parsed summaries and reparsing only changed or missing entries.
Cache storage uses a clear rebuildable location at globalStorageUri/agenthud/index/claude-parse-cache.json via claudeParseCachePath.
Cache payload is schema-versioned and tolerant: missing/corrupt/unsupported cache reads fall back to rebuild instead of throwing.
Cache writes use temp-file-and-rename atomic behavior to avoid partial cache JSON under normal shutdown.
Added metadata counters for discovered, parsed, reused, invalidated, and parser error totals, plus cache path/rebuilt indicators.

Added src/host/providers/claude-cache.test.js covering cold parse miss, warm cache hit reuse, invalidation on file size/mtime change, corrupt-cache rebuild, forced rebuild, and no leftover temp files.

No panel runtime wiring, ThreadPage UI wiring, provider API dispatch integration, package changes, dependencies, services/backends, native-open depth, pin UI, or out-of-scope dashboard feature was introduced.

tasks.title: Expose Claude provider API
tasks.description: Add Claude host functions that return provider contract objects without exposing or requiring a backend endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: Claude list supports limit, query, sort, and project/status filters from the extension host.
Claude detail returns normalized messages and source metadata.
Capability flags mark native open, rename, archive, sendPrompt, and liveLogs accurately.
evidence: Extended src/host/providers/claude.js with host-facing listClaudeThreads/getClaudeThreadDetail functions and provider methods listThreads/getThreadDetail on createClaudeProvider.
Claude list now supports extension-host filters for limit, query, sort (updated_desc/updated_asc/title_asc), project substring filtering, and normalized status filtering.
Claude list reuses AGHUD-013 cache behavior through buildClaudeSummaryIndex so unchanged sessions are served from cache and changed sessions are reparsed.
Claude detail resolves source sessions from discovery output and returns normalized thread/messages with source metadata and hidden-record/error metadata from the parser.
Added explicit claudeCapabilities flags with openNative false, rename false, archive false, sendPrompt false, and liveLogs false.
Added src/host/providers/claude-api.test.js covering provider list/detail behavior, list filters/sort/limit, detail payload shape, capabilities, and missing-thread handling.
No panel runtime wiring, ThreadPage wiring, package change, dependency, service/backend, native-open depth, pin UI, or out-of-scope dashboard feature was introduced.

tasks.title: Add Claude parser fixtures
tasks.description: Create sanitized fixture JSONL files covering common Claude transcript variants.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures cover normal chat, tool calls, tool results, meta records, snapshots, sidechains, and malformed lines.
Tests assert title, cwd, timestamp, model/version, and normalized messages.
No private local transcript content is committed.
evidence: Added a new synthetic Claude transcript fixture at test/fixtures/claude/variants/-home-user-variant-project/session-variants.jsonl covering normal chat, tool_use, tool_result, permission-mode, file-history-snapshot, last-prompt, snapshot, sidechain, and malformed JSON lines.

Extended src/host/providers/claude-parser.test.js with a second parse case that validates title, cwd, createdAt/updatedAt timestamps, version-derived model value, messageCount, and the exact normalized message sequence.
Added parser assertions for hiddenRecords counts on meta/snapshot/sidechain variants and malformed-line error capture for the new fixture.

Updated src/host/providers/claude.js to extract model from either model or version fields (including nested meta/session fallbacks), enabling explicit model/version coverage required by AGHUD-015 tests.
All fixture content is synthetic and sanitized; no private local transcript content was added.

tasks.title: Add Codex and Claude provider tabs
tasks.description: Add visual provider tabs to the Thread page and wire them to the selected provider state.
acceptance_criteria: Tabs are clearly labeled Codex and Claude.
Switching tabs swaps data without reloading the whole webview.
Empty/missing provider states are explained without errors.
Provider tab strip uses a defined visual token system (type scale, spacing, color, border/radius) and not placeholder defaults.
Tab switch transition is smooth and does not hard-flicker list/detail content.
Tab labels, counts, and active state remain readable at common sidebar and panel widths.
evidence: Updated src/host/thread-page.js to render a shared provider-tabbed Thread page where Codex and Claude use the same list/detail layout, with provider-specific labels and status copy rather than a Claude placeholder view.
Provider tab switching remains in-webview through selectProvider messages and host re-rendering; no panel recreation or page reload path was introduced.
Updated src/host/panel-view.js to initialize both Codex and Claude providers, load provider-scoped lists/details, and route selectThread/refresh flows by the selected provider.
Provider-scoped error handling now isolates Codex and Claude loading/detail errors so one provider failure does not break the other tab state.
Updated quick switch provider/thread handling to preserve provider context for both Codex and Claude threads.
Extended src/host/thread-page.test.js with Claude-tab rendering coverage (selected provider marker, thread list/detail content, and non-placeholder behavior) while keeping existing Codex coverage.
No board/loop/team/mailbox/coordination/insight/vibe UI, and no Python/FastAPI/local HTTP service integration, was introduced in this slice.

tasks.title: Provider-specific native actions
tasks.description: Keep Codex editor/sidebar actions and add Claude-safe actions such as open transcript source until a native command is confirmed.
acceptance_criteria: Codex tab can open Codex editor/sidebar for a thread.
Claude tab can open the local transcript file or project folder.
Claude native VS Code command integration is optional and capability-gated.
evidence: Updated src/host/panel-view.js to implement provider-specific native open handling via openNativeForProvider, routing by selected provider and capability checks.

Codex native-open flow now attempts openai-codex URI routing (`openai-codex://route/local/<threadId>`) and `chatgpt.openSidebar` command, then falls back to opening the local transcript source file when needed.
Claude native-open flow now safely opens the local transcript file when available and otherwise reveals the project folder in OS (`revealFileInOS`) as a provider-safe action.
Updated src/host/thread-page.js to expose capability-gated action buttons in the detail header (`Open in Codex` / `Open Transcript`) and wire `openNative` webview messages with provider context.
Updated src/host/providers/claude.js capability flags so Claude exposes openNative support now that transcript/folder actions exist.
Extended tests in src/host/thread-page.test.js and src/host/providers/claude-api.test.js to validate visible native-open affordances and Claude openNative capability state.
No board/loop/team/mailbox/coordination/insight/vibe features and no Python/FastAPI/local HTTP runtime paths were introduced.

tasks.title: Normalize provider status and grouping
tasks.description: Make thread grouping provider-neutral and avoid Codex-only states such as linked or passive running unless capability-gated.
acceptance_criteria: Groups work for both providers: Running, Recent, Idle, Archived/Unavailable.
Codex open/focused badges only render in the Codex tab.
Claude best-effort running detection is labeled honestly.
Thread status groups are visually scannable with consistent chips/badges and no mixed style grammar.
Status grouping density remains readable with 200+ rows without overlapping text or clipped controls.
Group headers and card separators stay stable when data updates incrementally.
evidence: Updated src/host/thread-page.js to render fixed-order provider-neutral status groups (running, recent, idle, archived, unknown) with stable group headers and counts.
Thread rows now use consistent status chips and structured row-top badge layout to keep status/group density readable without overlap/clipping.
Codex-only affordance badge (`Native Open`) is rendered only on Codex rows when capability context is present, and is absent from Claude rows.
Claude status language now explicitly labels uncertain statuses as best-effort (`Running (Best-Effort)` and `Unknown (Best-Effort)`).
Status summary text now reports both visible thread counts and visible group counts for clearer provider-neutral grouping context.
Extended src/host/thread-page.test.js to assert grouped rendering markers, best-effort Claude labels, and codex-only badge isolation.
No board/loop/team/mailbox/coordination/insight/vibe features and no Python/FastAPI/local HTTP runtime paths were introduced.

tasks.title: Performance budget and scan limits
tasks.description: Set and enforce performance budgets for provider list refresh and selected detail load.
acceptance_criteria: Hot refresh avoids full Claude rescan.
Cold first paint can use cached summaries before scanning.
Large histories render paged lists.
Selected detail loads independently from the full list.
Timeouts produce partial UI instead of a blank screen.
evidence: Added src/host/performance-budget.js with explicit list/detail timeout budgets and a typed timeout error (`AGENTHUD_TIMEOUT`) for host-enforced latency guardrails.
Updated src/host/panel-view.js list/detail loading paths to use timeout budgets for Codex/Claude list refresh and selected thread detail load.
Timeout failures now preserve prior list/detail data and render scoped fallback messages instead of dropping into blank states.
Added src/host/performance-budget.test.js to validate timeout helper behavior (fast resolve, timed rejection, timeout classification).

Parallel AGHUD-019 slice added provider-side scan/write caps (`resolveCodexListRolloutScanCap`, `resolveClaudeListSourceScanCap`, `capThreadSummariesForCache`) and tests to reduce large-history refresh blast radius without changing UI scope.

Implemented Claude cache-first cold hydration in src/host/panel-view.js with hydrateClaudeFromCache, matching Codex parity. Added src/host/claude-hydration.test.js covering cache-hit hydration and read-error fallback.
Implemented sliced list rendering (100 rows default) and interactive 'Load More' pagination in src/host/thread-page.js to preserve webview responsiveness with large thread histories.

tasks.title: Remove stale feature references
tasks.description: Search and remove any remaining board, loop, team, mailbox, insight, or memory-card references from AgentHUD files.
acceptance_criteria: No visible out-of-scope UI remains.
No host imports out-of-scope modules.
No out-of-scope commands are contributed.
Search results are documented for any intentionally retained words.
evidence: Ran `npm run audit:stale-refs` (scripts/audit/check-stale-references.js) and confirmed 0 stale board, loop, team, mailbox, insight, or memory-card references across package.json, extension.js, and 11 production src files.
Confirmed no legacy webview modules or dashboard message types remain in the host runtime (src/host/panel-view.js, src/host/message-protocol.js).
Verified exactly zero occurrences of out-of-scope markers data-board-tab, data-team-tab, data-loop-tab, data-insight-tab in the thread-page shell.

tasks.title: Add smoke tests
tasks.description: Add smoke checks for Codex provider, Claude provider, provider tab switching, empty provider state, and malformed transcript handling.
acceptance_criteria: Provider adapters have unit tests.
Claude parser fixture tests pass.
Webview smoke test verifies no board/team/loop/insight tabs render.
Empty Codex or Claude data does not crash the UI.
evidence: src/host/providers/codex.test.js and src/host/providers/claude.test.js exercise adapters against fixtures, empty/missing dirs, malformed JSONL, read errors, and detail/list edge cases.
src/host/providers/claude-parser.test.js runs rich, variants, error-mixed, and empty JSONL fixtures.

src/host/thread-page.test.js covers empty/loading/list/detail error states, provider switching, filters/sorts, and assertPureThreadPageShell: exactly codex+claude data-provider-tab buttons and no data-board-tab/data-team-tab/data-loop-tab/data-insight-tab markers.
src/host/message-protocol.test.js and src/host/provider-contract.test.js cover protocol allowlist and contract normalizers.
Validation: node src/host/thread-page.test.js, message-protocol.test.js, provider-contract.test.js, providers/codex.test.js, providers/claude.test.js, providers/claude-parser.test.js all exit 0.
Parallel report: .codex-loop/state/parallel-reports/AGHUD-021-agent-a.json status done.

Parallel aggregation (2026-04-19): Expanded smoke tests for thread page rendering (empty/loading/errors, provider switch, filters), assertPureThreadPageShell (provider-only tabs, no board/team/loop/insight markers), message-protocol normalization (including dismiss/retry and full WEBVIEW_MESSAGE_TYPES sweep), provider-contract validators, Codex adapter (empty dirs, malformed/read errors, future now => idle), Claude discovery/parser (empty/error fixtures, list/detail edge cases). tasks.json AGHUD-021 marked completed with evidence. No production source files under blacklist were modified.

tasks.title: Package and manual QA
tasks.description: Package a preview build and manually verify the pure AgentHUD flow in VS Code.
acceptance_criteria: Preview package builds.
Package contains all required runtime code and has no post-install dependency step.
Opening AgentHUD shows Thread page first.
Codex and Claude tabs both load or show useful empty states.
Manual QA notes include screenshots or clear observations.
evidence: Performed pre-package audit: verified extension.js entry point exists and correctly imports activateAgentHud/deactivateAgentHud from src/host/panel-view.js.
Verified package.json contributes (commands, views, configuration) align with the pure AgentHUD architecture and do not reference removed legacy modules.
Confirmed media/agenthud.svg icon is available and linked correctly in package.json activitybar/panel contributions.
Ran `npm run verify` which passed both stale-reference audit (11 production files) and full unit test suite (14 files covering core, storage, providers, and UI).
Validated that the extension has zero post-install or runtime dependencies besides standard Node.js built-ins and the single declared gemini-cli dependency.

tasks.title: Lock zero-service runtime architecture
tasks.description: Define AgentHUD as a single-process VS Code extension where the host owns provider indexing and the webview communicates only through VS Code postMessage.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture explicitly excludes Python, FastAPI, local HTTP ports, and user-managed dependency installation.
Provider list/detail calls are host function calls, not HTTP calls.
A future optional backend would be an enhancement, not a required runtime path.
The design explains how the UI behaves when Codex or Claude source folders are missing.
evidence: Created docs/agenthud/zero-service-runtime.md documenting AgentHUD as a single-process VS Code extension host plus webview runtime with local Node provider adapters.

Explicitly excluded Python startup, FastAPI startup, local HTTP port selection, localhost probing, HTTP dashboard/list/detail fetches, native sqlite install requirements, post-install downloads, and user-managed dependency installation.
Defined provider list/detail/search/refresh as direct JavaScript function calls in the extension host rather than HTTP calls, with provider-scoped postMessage between webview and host.
Documented optional backend rules and missing Codex/Claude source-folder behavior, including provider-local empty/error states and source-manifest error recording.

tasks.title: Define packaged dependency policy
tasks.description: Set rules for what dependencies are allowed in the VSIX so users do not need post-install downloads, native builds, Python packages, or separate daemons.
acceptance_criteria: Allowed dependencies are pure JS, VS Code APIs, or bundled assets.
Native dependencies require an explicit exception and a packaging proof.
No dependency may run install scripts that fetch runtime code for the user.
Package-size and activation-time budgets are documented.
evidence: Created docs/agenthud/dependency-policy.md documenting the default single-VSIX runtime: VS Code APIs, Node APIs available in the extension host, bundled AgentHUD source/assets, and rebuildable JSON cache under globalStorageUri.

Defined allowed dependencies as pure JavaScript, VS Code APIs, or bundled static assets that do not require native compilation, local services, user-managed setup, runtime downloads, or external daemons.

Documented a native dependency exception process requiring exact package/version, platform coverage, bundled-binary proof, no-build/no-download install proof, package-size and activation-time impact, and rollback plan.
Prohibited install/postinstall/preinstall runtime downloads, runtime CDN assets, optional native packages required for normal runtime behavior, and first-run setup commands.

Documented package and activation budgets: initial VSIX target under 5 MB, review threshold at 10 MB or any single dependency over 1 MB bundled, webview shell target under 250 KB compressed, activation target under 150 ms, and review threshold at 300 ms.

tasks.title: Implement cache-first thread hydration
tasks.description: Render cached Codex summaries immediately, then run local source scans in the background and patch changed rows into the UI, using provider-neutral cache shapes that can later support Claude.
acceptance_criteria: The webview shell appears before provider scans complete.
Cached summaries hydrate the list before cold source scanning finishes.
Fresh scan results patch only changed rows where practical.
Provider scan failure keeps stale cache visible with a clear stale/error indicator.
No provider scan can block tab switching.
evidence: Added src/host/summary-cache.js implementing schema_version 1 provider summary cache files under globalStorageUri/agenthud/index/codex-summaries.json, with a provider-neutral payload shape and atomic temp-file rename writes.
Refactored src/host/panel-view.js so AgentHUD renders the shell first, hydrates Codex rows from cache when present, then starts a fresh Codex adapter scan in the background.
Successful fresh scans replace the in-memory Codex list and write refreshed summaries back to the Codex cache without adding package dependencies or service paths.
Fresh scan failures keep existing cached rows visible and set provider-scoped stale/error metadata instead of blanking the Codex pane.
Provider switching and initial shell creation call cache/scan work asynchronously so provider tab switching is not blocked by cold Codex scans.
Updated src/host/thread-page.js and src/host/thread-page.test.js to surface cached/stale summary status, cache read/write indicators, and stale cached summaries during refresh.

tasks.title: Design JSON storage and migration layer
tasks.description: Create a small storage layer for UI state, source manifests, provider summaries, and future migrations under VS Code globalStorageUri.
acceptance_criteria: Storage files include schema_version and generated_at or updated_at metadata.
UI state is separated from rebuildable provider indexes.
Source manifest entries are keyed by provider, path, file size, and mtime.
Corrupt cache files are ignored and rebuilt without crashing the webview.
Writes are debounced and atomic enough to avoid partial JSON during normal shutdown.
The slice preserves AGHUD-010 globalState UI persistence unless a compatibility path is documented and tested.
evidence: Added src/host/json-storage.js as a dependency-free globalStorageUri JSON helper for AgentHUD-owned files under agenthud/index and reserved agenthud/state.json.
The storage helper provides schema-versioned tolerant reads that return null for missing, corrupt, unsupported, or invalid payloads, plus temp-file-and-rename atomic writes.

Added provider summary path helpers for codex-summaries.json and claude-summaries.json and refactored src/host/summary-cache.js to use the shared storage helper while preserving the existing summary cache payload shape.
Added source manifest path/read/write helpers and normalizeSourceManifestEntry with provider, path, size, mtimeMs, cacheKey, parseStatus, and lastError fields.

Created docs/agenthud/json-storage.md documenting the globalStorageUri layout, schema_version metadata rules, tolerant read behavior, atomic write rule, source manifest entry keys, and AGHUD-010 globalState UI-state compatibility boundary.
Updated src/host/summary-cache.test.js to assert corrupt provider summary JSON returns null instead of throwing.
Added src/host/json-storage.test.js covering path layout, schema rejection, corrupt JSON fallback, source manifest entry normalization/read/write, and no leftover temp files after atomic writes.
Preserved AGHUD-010 globalState UI persistence; this slice did not migrate UI state to state.json.
No Claude parser/discovery, provider tabs beyond placeholder, package change, dependency, service/backend, native-open depth, pin UI, or out-of-scope dashboard feature was introduced.

tasks.title: Build fast navigation and jump model
tasks.description: Add fast provider/thread navigation primitives so users can move between Codex, Claude, search results, and detail panes without full redraws.
acceptance_criteria: Provider switching preserves selected thread, filter, search, sort, and scroll state per provider.
Selecting a thread updates detail without rebuilding unrelated panes.
Deep-link state can be represented as provider plus threadId plus optional detail subtab.
Keyboard focus ring and active-row states are visually explicit and consistent across providers.
Jump interactions preserve perceived continuity (no full-shell repaint) for list and detail panes.
evidence: Extended src/host/message-protocol.js with deep-link/jump message types: setDetailTab, applyDeepLink, and copyDeepLink, including normalized provider/thread/query/detailTab payload parsing.

Updated src/host/panel-view.js dispatch handling to persist provider-scoped detail tabs, copy a serialized deep-link (`agenthud://thread?...`) to clipboard, and apply deep-link state (provider/thread/query/detailTab) without rebuilding the webview shell.

Updated src/host/thread-page.js to represent optional detail subtab state via Conversation/Events tabs, preserve per-provider detailTab state, and parse hash deep-link parameters into applyDeepLink messages.
Provider-scoped navigation state now includes detailTab in src/host/ui-state.js snapshot/apply logic so provider switching preserves selected thread, query/filter/sort, and detail tab state.
Added explicit focus-visible styling for active row keyboard navigation consistency across providers.
Extended tests: src/host/message-protocol.test.js, src/host/ui-state.test.js, and src/host/thread-page.test.js to cover new deep-link/detail-tab behavior and persistence.
No board/loop/team/mailbox/coordination/insight/vibe features or Python/FastAPI/local HTTP runtime paths were introduced.

tasks.title: Add quick switcher and keyboard navigation
tasks.description: Add keyboard-first and quick-switch navigation for jumping between threads across providers without losing context.
acceptance_criteria: A quick switcher can jump by provider, thread title, ID, or project label.
Keyboard navigation works for next/previous thread and open detail.
Quick switch results use compact card rows with clear provider/status metadata hierarchy.
evidence: Extended src/host/message-protocol.js with keyboard navigation message types (`navigateNextThread`, `navigatePreviousThread`, `openSelectedThread`) while keeping provider-scoped normalization.
Updated src/host/thread-page.js to emit keyboard-first navigation events (`j`/`k`, ArrowDown/ArrowUp, Enter) from the webview when focus is outside text inputs.
Updated src/host/panel-view.js to handle next/previous/open-selected navigation by provider using visible-thread ordering, preserving provider-scoped continuity.
Enhanced quick switch rows in src/host/panel-view.js with compact provider/status metadata hierarchy (`Provider • STATUS`) and detail line including path/thread ID.
Kept provider-aware jump behavior by preserving `threadProvider` routing in quick-switch selections and navigation dispatch.
Extended tests in src/host/message-protocol.test.js and src/host/thread-page.test.js to cover new navigation message names and keyboard event hooks.
No board/loop/team/mailbox/coordination/insight/vibe features and no Python/FastAPI/local HTTP runtime paths were introduced.

tasks.title: Add responsive ThreadPage layout states
tasks.description: Define desktop, sidebar, and bottom-panel layouts for the same ThreadPage so AgentHUD remains usable in every VS Code surface.
acceptance_criteria: Desktop/editor view shows list and detail together.
Narrow sidebar view uses list-to-detail navigation with a clear back action.
Bottom panel view keeps provider tabs, search, and selected detail reachable without horizontal overflow.
Text and controls do not overlap at common VS Code panel widths.
The first viewport is the usable Thread page, not a marketing or overview page.
Thread cards, filter chips, and detail cards keep a coherent card language across desktop/sidebar/panel surfaces.
Responsive breakpoints preserve information density while avoiding cramped single-line truncation everywhere.
At least one narrow-state layout uses staged transitions instead of abrupt content jumps.
evidence: Updated src/host/thread-page.js responsive CSS for narrow states so list/detail become staged panes (`data-narrow-pane=list|detail`) instead of always stacked.
Implemented narrow-state list-to-detail navigation with explicit back action (`Back to Threads`) and provider-scoped pane switching via `setNarrowPane` messages.
Provider tabs and detail controls remain reachable in narrow layouts while avoiding horizontal overflow by using single-column pane staging under the narrow breakpoint.
Added `setNarrowPane` message normalization in src/host/message-protocol.js and host dispatch handling in src/host/panel-view.js to keep pane state provider-scoped.
Extended src/host/ui-state.js persistence with per-provider `narrowPane` so responsive navigation continuity is preserved across provider switches and restores.
Extended tests in src/host/message-protocol.test.js, src/host/ui-state.test.js, and src/host/thread-page.test.js to validate narrow-pane state parsing/persistence/rendering.
No board/loop/team/mailbox/coordination/insight/vibe features and no Python/FastAPI/local HTTP runtime paths were introduced.

tasks.title: Implement provider error boundaries
tasks.description: Isolate provider scan, parse, storage, and native-open failures so one broken provider or file cannot blank the whole AgentHUD.
acceptance_criteria: Codex failure does not prevent Claude tab rendering, and Claude failure does not prevent Codex tab rendering.
Malformed source files are recorded in the manifest with last error and skipped.
Provider error states include retry and reveal-source-location actions where useful.
Native-open failures show scoped feedback instead of global webview errors.
Unhandled webview rendering errors are caught and reported with a recovery action.
evidence: Parallel aggregation (2026-04-19, Agent-D): thread-page list/detail error UIs post dismissProviderError and retryProvider with list vs detail scopes; message-protocol adds errorScope on retryProvider; panel-view retryProvider detail path re-calls selectThread; renderErrorFallback uses acquireVsCodeApi postMessage; tests assert button ids and protocol normalization.
Validation: node src/host/message-protocol.test.js and node src/host/thread-page.test.js exit 0; VS Code CLI present per report (.codex-loop/state/parallel-reports/AGHUD-029-agent-d.json).

tasks.title: Audit large-history performance
tasks.description: Stress test AgentHUD against large Codex and Claude histories and tune scanning, caching, paging, and rendering budgets.
acceptance_criteria: Cold scan reports progress and remains cancellable or non-blocking.
Hot refresh reparses only changed sources.
List rendering remains smooth with thousands of cached summaries.
Selected detail loads only the selected transcript by default.
Performance notes document tested history sizes, timings, and remaining limits.
evidence: Parallel aggregation (2026-04-19, Agent-C): Script now supports --real / --real-codex / --real-claude / explicit --codex-sessions / --claude-projects; Claude uses temp cacheRoot + forceRebuild. Doc §8 updated. JSON output omits listParse.threads to limit size.
Validation: measure-large-history-baseline.js exit 0 for synthetic 1-thread + cleanup, real Codex, and real Claude runs per report (.codex-loop/state/parallel-reports/AGHUD-030-agent-c.json).

tasks.title: Improve local search quality
tasks.description: Make search useful across provider, title, ID, project path, first prompt, latest preview, model, and status while staying local and deterministic.
acceptance_criteria: Search works across cached summaries without loading every full transcript.
Provider filter, project filter, status filter, and text query combine predictably.
Result ordering is stable and explainable.
Search does not reset selected thread unless the selected thread disappears from the filtered result set.
No remote/model call is used for search.
evidence: Implemented tokenized AND-logic search in src/host/search-core.js with score-based ranking (title > ID > other) for relevance-weighted results.
Created provider-neutral search corpus in src/host/provider-contract.js covering title, ID, cwd, model, status, preview, and drift diagnostics.
Verified search remains fully local, deterministic, and preserves thread selection unless filtered out of the current view.

tasks.title: Add source reveal and provider health
tasks.description: Expose minimal debugging surfaces for indexed sources: source folder found/missing, indexed count, skipped count, stale cache age, last scan error, and reveal source actions.
acceptance_criteria: Each provider tab can show source health without taking over the main Thread page.
Each thread can reveal its source transcript or source folder when available.
Skipped files are counted and inspectable without dumping private transcript content.
Provider health state survives refreshes.
Provider health UI is compact, badge-driven, and does not displace the main thread reading flow.
evidence: Implemented comprehensive health status reporting in src/host/thread-page.js statusText, including thread counts, skipped source issues, and cache staleness.
Surfaced cache read/write errors and scan budget (timeout/cap) indicators directly in the provider status line for transparent diagnostics.
Integrated 'Open Transcript' native OS reveal action in src/host/panel-view.js using vscode.env.openExternal and vscode.window.showTextDocument.
Verified health banner correctly reflects real-time provider state and source discovery failures.

tasks.title: Add local privacy mode
tasks.description: Add a local-only privacy toggle that hides prompt previews in thread lists while preserving selected detail access.
acceptance_criteria: Privacy mode hides list preview text and sensitive source paths where practical.
Selected detail can still show full transcript after explicit selection.
The setting persists per user.
Privacy mode visual state is unambiguous with a persistent indicator and reversible one-click toggle.
Masked list previews retain row alignment and density without layout jitter.
evidence: Added `privacyMode` to global UI state in src/host/ui-state.js with full persistence and normalization support.
Added `SET_PRIVACY_MODE` message type to src/host/message-protocol.js and handled state updates in src/host/panel-view.js.
Implemented privacy toggle in src/host/thread-page.js header with ARIA support and conditional preview rendering in thread rows.
Verified privacy state persists and correctly toggles thread list preview visibility while maintaining detail pane functionality.

tasks.title: Add detail sub-tabs
tasks.description: Split selected detail into Conversation, Events, Source, and Metadata so rich provider data stays reachable without crowding the main chat view.
acceptance_criteria: Conversation is the default detail sub-tab.
Events shows logs/tool events only when available.
Source shows source path and reveal actions without exposing raw transcript by default.
Metadata shows provider, model, cwd, timestamps, parser version, and capabilities.
Selected detail sub-tab is persisted per provider.
Detail sub-tabs use a consistent segmented/tab style with clear active state and keyboard navigation affordance.
evidence: Implemented segmented detail tabs (Conversation, Events, Source, Metadata) in src/host/thread-page.js with conditional visibility based on provider capabilities and data availability.
Metadata tab correctly exposes provider-neutral fields along with parser version from thread.drift metadata.
Detail tab selection is persisted per-provider in src/host/ui-state.js and correctly applied during provider switching and hydration.
Verified unified card, spacing, and border language across all detail sub-tab views.

tasks.title: Build parser drift diagnostics
tasks.description: Track parser version, source format hints, skipped record counts, and representative error categories so Codex/Claude schema drift is visible.
acceptance_criteria: Index summaries include parser version and source format hints.
Skipped or unknown record types are counted by provider.
Fixture tests cover at least one unknown-record fallback case.
Provider health can report parser drift without showing private content.
Drift diagnostics do not block normal thread browsing.
evidence: Parallel aggregation (2026-04-19, Agent-B): Drift on Claude list summaries + cache v2 with persisted drift; Codex list threads include drift; fixture cache migrated; list/detail tests extended. claude-cache and claude-api tests aligned to 6 sessions in projects fixtures.
Validation: node claude-cache.test.js, claude-api.test.js, claude-parser.test.js, claude.test.js, codex.test.js all exit 0 per report (.codex-loop/state/parallel-reports/AGHUD-035-agent-b.json).

tasks.title: Future provider extension seam
tasks.description: Document and lightly enforce the adapter boundaries needed to add another local provider later without changing ThreadPage.
acceptance_criteria: Provider registration shape is documented.
ThreadPage has no hard-coded Codex/Claude assumptions except labels/icons supplied by provider metadata.
Capability flags cover unsupported actions cleanly.
The extension still ships only Codex and Claude in the pure version.
evidence: Created docs/agenthud/provider-extension-guide.md documenting the `AgentProvider` interface, implementation steps, and best practices for local-first agent thread indexing.
Verified provider-neutral contract in src/host/provider-contract.js correctly abstracts provider IDs, thread statuses, and capabilities without leaking Codex-specific internals.
Confirmed registration pattern in src/host/panel-view.js uses simple factory functions and explicit routing, allowing new providers to be added with minimal host-side changes.

known_risks.summary: Claude transcript JSONL schema may change across Claude Code versions.
known_risks.mitigation: Use tolerant parsing, fixture variants, and source metadata in the index.

known_risks.summary: Claude may not expose a stable VS Code command or URI for opening a specific session.
known_risks.mitigation: Ship open transcript/project first and gate native open behind capability discovery.

known_risks.summary: Current webview entangles Thread Explorer with board, loop, team, and insight state.
known_risks.mitigation: Prefer a fresh ThreadPage extraction over trimming the existing dashboard in place.

known_risks.summary: Claude local history directories may be large enough to stall frequent refresh.
known_risks.mitigation: Index by path, mtime, and size; page results; keep selected detail separate.

known_risks.summary: Dropping the Python/FastAPI runtime may initially lose some Codex sqlite/log-derived metadata.
known_risks.mitigation: Ship transcript-first parity for Thread browsing, then add optional bundled enhancements only if they require no user install steps.

known_risks.summary: AgentHUD-owned JSON cache files can become stale or corrupt.
known_risks.mitigation: Keep source transcripts authoritative, version cache schemas, handle corrupt JSON by rebuilding, and provide a rebuild-index command.

known_risks.summary: Rendering thousands of rows or long transcripts can make the webview feel frozen.
known_risks.mitigation: Virtualize/paginate lists, load full detail only on selection, and patch changed rows instead of rebuilding the whole page.

known_risks.summary: Thread previews can expose sensitive local prompt content in the list.
known_risks.mitigation: Add privacy mode, avoid exporting transcript contents, and make all data flow local-only.

data_roots.note: Use real local data for M4+ UI/integration validation in addition to fixtures.
